templates.name: Metroidvania (2D side-scroller)
templates.description: 2D metroidvania base: MetSys world map/minimap with a 2-room starter map, platformer player (coyote time, jump buffer, variable jump height), automatic room transitions, cell discovery, and the standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager groups, save_data() contract).

vendoredAddons.name: MetroidvaniaSystem
vendoredAddons.version: 1.5 (branch 4.6 — repo has no release tags; master tracks Godot 4.7-dev)

patches.replace:
if not ResourceUID.has_id(ResourceUID.text_to_id("uid://crxgu2q73va5y")):
		# Bootstrap import (godot --headless --import): the UID cache is not
		# built yet, so the editor UI cannot load. Skip it; the plugin loads
		# fully on the next editor start.
		return
	main = load("uid://crxgu2q73va5y").instantiate()
patches.reason: skip editor-UI creation during bootstrap --import runs where the UID cache does not exist yet

patches.replace:
if not is_instance_valid(main):
		return
	main.queue_free()
	remove_tool_menu_item("MetSys")
patches.reason: guard _exit_tree teardown for bootstrap/instant-quit editor runs where the UI was never created

primitives.note: the platformer action set is already baked into the skeleton's project.godot; re-run only to add gamepad rebinding UI
the player carries a Camera2D whose limits are clamped to the current MetSys room; swap in the sidescroller rig for deadzone/look-ahead
player controller already implements coyote time + jump buffering; use game-feel for shake/hitstop when combat is added

assetPlanHints: Player character spritesheet (~32x48 px, idle/run/jump/fall, pixel art)
Tileset for rooms (16px or 32px grid, tileable: ground, walls, platforms)
Parallax background layers (2-3, per biome)
Door / passage frame sprite (marks MetSys border type 1)
Map theme override art only if the bundled MetSys 'Exquisite' minimap theme clashes with the palette

templates.name: Point-and-Click Adventure
templates.description: Classic point-and-click adventure base on Popochiu: one starter room with a clickable prop and a walkable area, a player character wired as PC, the simple_click GUI (look/interact verbs, inventory bar, dialog lines), transition layer, and Popochiu's room/character/inventory/dialog pipeline ready in the editor dock.

primitives.note: generate a theme.tres from the game palette and apply it to game/gui/gui.tscn to de-generic the Popochiu GUI
room/prop descriptions and dialog trees; Popochiu dialogs live in game/dialogs/

assetPlanHints: Room background painting (320x180 px, VGA-adventure look; one per room)
Player character walk/idle/talk spritesheets (4-direction or side view, ~32x64 px)
Prop sprites with readable silhouettes (transparent PNG, placed on the background)
Cursor sprite set (active/look/wait variants)
Inventory item icons (16x16 or 32x32 px)

templates.name: FPS / Immersive Sim (3D)
templates.description: First-person immersive-sim base on COGITO: full player controller (sprint/crouch/slide/stairs/ladders, attributes: health/stamina/visibility-stealth, grid inventory, quick slots, wieldables), component-based interaction system, quest system, EasyMenus main/pause menus with save slots — plus one blockout test room with the COGITO player, an openable door and a carryable health-potion pickup wired as live interactables. Engine pin note (tested 2026-07-11): COGITO v1.1.6 targets Godot 4.5.1 and imports clean there; on 4.6.1 its scene manager fails to parse (GDScript analyzer cannot resolve CogitoQuestManager.active/completed/failed typed members, breaking SaveSlotButton.gd) — so this template pins 4.5 and must be scaffolded/validated with a 4.5.x Godot. Standard NoxDev ABI on top (Master/Music/SFX buses by name, player/game_manager groups, save_data() contract on GameManager, pause action alongside COGITO's menu action).

vendoredAddons.name: COGITO
vendoredAddons.version: 1.1.6 (Codeberg is the live upstream — GitHub mirror's tags stop at v1.1.5; targets Godot 4.5.1-stable)

patches.reason: upstream ships no .import for SlotBorder.png, so fresh imports mint a new UID and the committed one warns on every boot; drop the stale UID and load by path

vendoredAddons.name: Input Helper (bundled with COGITO)
vendoredAddons.version: 4.6.0 (nathanhoad/godot_input_helper as vendored inside COGITO v1.1.6 — same pin so versions can never skew)

primitives.note: COGITO's full action set (32 actions, keyboard + gamepad) is baked into the skeleton's project.godot and Input Helper provides runtime rebinding — do not re-emit the fps template over it; use this skill only for reference when adding new actions
the test room is a hand-built blockout; use world-layout for level graybox passes, keep layer 1 Environment / layer 2 Interactables
COGITO ships headbob/fall-damage; use game-feel for hit feedback and camera kick when combat wieldables are added
COGITO's dynamic footstep system reads surface types — feed it material-tagged surfaces; buses are ABI-standard Master/Music/SFX

assetPlanHints: Environment texture set (walls/floor/ceiling, tileable PBR, 2m repeat)
Interactable prop models (door frame + leaf, containers, switches; GLB with collision)
Pickup/inventory item models + 64x64 inventory icons (COGITO items pair a 3D pickup with an icon)
First-person wieldable models (hands + tool/weapon, ~5k tris)
UI substitutions for the COGITO HUD theme (crosshair/reticle, attribute bar styling)
Ambience loops + surface-tagged footstep sets (concrete/metal/wood)

templates.name: Visual Novel (with dice checks)
templates.description: Visual-novel base on Dialogue Manager 3 plus a pen-and-paper dice layer: title scene, story scene with blockout stage, a VN textbox (name plate, typewriter DialogueLabel, response menu, click/accept to advance), one .dialogue file with a branch and a skill-check gate, and a SkillCheck autoload (d20 + stat modifier vs DC, crit rules, seedable RNG) whose dice-roll popup is awaited as a Dialogue Manager mutation (`do SkillCheck.skill_check("mind", 12)` then `if SkillCheck.last_success`). Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, pause action).

primitives.note: story content lives in dialogue/*.dialogue — Dialogue Manager syntax (titles, responses, conditions, mutations); the skill_check gate in intro.dialogue is the pattern to copy for new checks
generate a theme.tres from the game palette and set it on the textbox Panel, title screen, and dice popup to de-generic the default gray look
VN portrait pipelines (talking portraits, expression sets) for the Stage placeholders in story.tscn

assetPlanHints: Background paintings (1280x720, one per scene/location)
Character portraits (~400x600 px, transparent PNG; neutral + 2-3 expressions each)
Textbox 9-patch panel + name plate art
Title screen key art (1280x720)
d20 die face art or spritesheet for the dice popup (replaces the number label)
UI SFX (advance blip, dice tumble, success/failure stingers)

templates.name: Bullet Hell / Shmup
templates.description: Bullet-hell base on BulletUpHell: the Spawning autoload (pooled shape-based bullets in a shared Area2D), one arena with a spawner firing an infinite 12-way ring pattern (PatternCircle, 0.8s volleys), a player ship with 8-dir movement, focus-slowdown, small hurtbox, lives/invulnerability/respawn wired to BulletUpHell's collision signal, HUD (lives + live bullet count), and the standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, topdown input action set).

vendoredAddons.name: BulletUpHell
vendoredAddons.version: 4.4 (branch Demo-V4.4 @ pinned SHA — the default branch and the maintained Godot-4 line; release tags stop at 4.2.3)

patches.reason: upstream ships no .import files, so fresh imports assign new texture UIDs; drop the stale committed UID and load by path (silences per-boot invalid-UID warnings)

primitives.note: the topdown action set is baked into the skeleton's project.godot ('dash' doubles as shmup focus-slowdown); re-run only for rebinding UI
bullet spawn/despawn flashes and player death bursts; BulletUpHell handles bullet visuals, vfx covers everything around them
screen shake on player hit and hitstop on death — hook player.lives_changed / died

assetPlanHints: Player ship sprite (~32x32 px, readable silhouette, subtle thruster animation)
Enemy spawner / boss sprite (~64x64 px)
Bullet sprites (8-16 px, high-contrast: round shot, needle shot; BulletUpHell swaps them via animState resources)
Scrolling background (vertical, tileable, low-contrast so bullets stay readable)
Hit/death explosion flipbook (small, additive)

templates.name: Deckbuilder (Slay-the-Spire-like)
templates.description: Deckbuilder combat base on chun92/card-framework: JSON-defined cards (5 definitions: Strike/Cleave/Defend/Insight/Surge with generated blockout art), a 10-card starting deck, and a full draw/play/discard loop — 5-card hand refill, 3 energy per turn, drag-a-card-to-the-play-area to resolve its effect (damage/block/draw/energy), discard reshuffles into the deck when it runs dry, End Turn discards the hand and a dummy enemy attacks through your block; victory/defeat banners with restart. Kit decision (2026-07-11): DesirePathGames/Slay-The-Robot was evaluated per the roadmap but it is a complete game (game scenes/autoloads/data at repo root, no addon payload to vendor) — kept as an MIT reference for meta-structure (JSON card mods, run structure); card-framework is the vendorable engine (containers, drag-drop, JSON factory; Godot 4.6 pin upstream).

vendoredAddons.name: Card Framework
vendoredAddons.version: 1.4.0 (upstream targets Godot 4.6+; script-only kit, no editor plugin)

patches.replace: pass # NoxDev: upstream printed every preloaded card here — one line per card per boot
patches.reason: silence per-card preload prints that flood the log on every boot

primitives.note: theme the table HUD (stats, End Turn, banners) from the game palette; the card faces are textures — restyle those via image-pipeline
replace the generated blockout card art in cards/art/ (150x210 per card + card_back) — filenames are referenced from cards/data/*.json
juice the resolve moments: shake/flash on enemy hit, punch-scale on the banner — hook play_area.card_played

assetPlanHints: Card face art (150x210 px per card: frame + illustration window + name/description bands)
Card back (150x210 px, deck identity pattern)
Enemy portraits (~256x256 px, one per encounter)
Table background (1280x720, low-contrast felt/board so cards stay readable)
UI icons (energy bolt, block shield, HP heart, draw/discard pile markers)
Card SFX (draw swish, play thunk, shuffle riffle, enemy hit)

templates.name: Top-Down Action (Hotline-Miami-like)
templates.description: Top-down action base, pure first-party (no vendored kit — build-cheap per the roadmap survey): CharacterBody2D player with 8-directional movement, mouse aim, hitscan raycast shot with tracer, dash with cooldown and health/respawn; three shootable practice targets; a NavigationAgent2D chaser enemy pathing across the arena's NavigationRegion2D with contact damage; arena with walls, HUD (HP + targets left), and the standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, topdown input action set).

primitives.note: the topdown action set is already baked into the skeleton's project.godot; re-run only to add gamepad rebinding UI (right-stick aim is the main gamepad gap)
wire shake/hitstop into player.shot_fired and take_hit — the signals are already emitted
the arena is a single hand-built room; use world-layout to generate level floors, then re-bake each level's NavigationRegion2D

assetPlanHints: Player character top-down spritesheet (~32x32 px, idle/run/shoot, 8-direction or rotation-based)
Enemy top-down spritesheet (~32x32 px, walk cycle; palette-distinct from player)
Floor tileset (32px grid, tileable: concrete/wood/tile variants)
Wall tileset (32px grid, top-down with slight top face)
Muzzle flash + impact spark sprites (small, additive)
Practice target / destructible prop sprite (~32x32 px)

templates.name: Isometric ARPG (Diablo-like)
templates.description: Isometric action-RPG base, pure first-party (per the Wave-2 survey the genre's core loop is build-cheap): click-to-move player (NavigationAgent2D, hold-to-repath Diablo style) on an isometric TileMapLayer (diamond-down 128x64 tileset, floor painted in code from a 2-tile atlas), an ability bar with a melee swing and a cooldown AoE nova, three chasing enemies (the top-down-action chaser archetype), and a loot system: Pandora-style item database in data/items.json, weighted rarity roll (common/magic/rare/legendary) scaling the item's base stat, rarity-tinted ground pickups collected on walk-over into a run inventory. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, pause action).

primitives.note: hitstop on melee connects and shake on nova — hook player.ability_used and enemy take_hit flashes
the diamond arena is one hand-authored NavigationPolygon; use world-layout for dungeon floors, then re-author each floor's nav polygon around its walls
town NPCs / vendors import as Pandora entities — the same JSON style items.json already uses

assetPlanHints: Isometric floor tileset (128x64 diamond tiles: ground variants, cracked/decorated; replaces assets/tiles/iso_tiles.png)
Player character isometric spritesheet (~64x96 px, idle/walk/attack in 4 or 8 directions)
Enemy isometric spritesheets (~64x64 px, walk/attack; one silhouette per enemy family)
Loot pickup sprites (small item icons on the ground + 32x32 inventory icons per items.json entry)
Ability bar icons (melee swing, nova burst; 48x48 px)
Impact/nova VFX flipbooks (additive, short)
